title: midnight-craving german sauerbraten stew
description: tangy, creamy, german dish made with chopped onion, red wine, and a dash of spices. serves well as a midnight craving.
instructions: in a large bowl, combine beef, onion, red wine, apple cider vinegar, brown sugar, salt, black pepper, bay leaves, allspice, and caraway seed. marinate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
remove beef from marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
sear beef until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. transfer to a large Dutch oven or heavy pot.
add flour to the skillet, stirring to combine with butter. cook for 1 minute.
add heavy cream to the skillet, whisking to combine with flour and butter. bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 2 minutes.
pour the cream sauce over the beef in the Dutch oven.
cover the pot and transfer to the oven at 300 degrees F.
bake for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the beef is tender.
serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
serve with a side of steamed vegetables.
serve with a side of boiled potatoes.
